summary: defi-env-auditor exfiltrates host info and environment to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages C2
details: The package's postinstall script in package.json (line 31) and scanner.js perform host reconnaissance and exfiltration to a hardcoded attacker endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io. The lifecycle hook loads child_process, os, and https, gathers hostname, current working directory, user identity (whoami), and process.env, and POSTs the data to the GitHub Pages-hosted C2. scanner.js (lines 142-161, 234-249, 332-337) repeats the pattern with broader filesystem reads (fs.existsSync) and additional system-intel collection fields (hostname, cwd, version) before transmitting to the same domain. The fetch destination is unrelated to any legitimate package publisher, the package's stated purpose ('env auditor') is a cover story, and the install-time POST runs without user consent. This is an install-time data-exfiltration beacon — installer environment variables (which routinely contain API keys, cloud credentials, and tokens in dev/CI contexts) flow directly to attacker infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
